# promptgarten

> Free multilingual (de/en/es/fr/zh) learning platform for AI-assisted and agent-based coding: 111 glossary chapters with two depth levels, quizzes and exercises, a command reference covering 318 commands across 5 CLI platforms, a cross-platform command map, a troubleshooting catalog of real error messages, model comparisons by intelligence-per-dollar, learning paths, a daily quiz, prompt templates, add-ons, and a curated news feed. Every factual claim carries a visible, verified source. All content is intentionally AI-friendly and scrape-friendly — free to reuse with a link back. No account required.

All content is available as free JSON — no auth, no rate limits beyond fair use. AI agents welcome.

## Free JSON API

Every endpoint exists per language: replace `.en.` with `.de.` `.es.` `.fr.` or `.zh.`

- [API index](/api/index.json): lists every endpoint with a description
- [Entries](/api/entries.en.json): 111 learning chapters — title, teaser, body (beginner) + bodyDetail (deep), difficulty 1-3, quiz, exercise with self-check, sources
- [Commands](/api/commands.en.json): 318 CLI/slash commands across Claude Code, Cursor CLI, Antigravity CLI, Aider, Codex CLI — what each does, when it fits, when it does not, sources
- [Rosetta](/api/rosetta.en.json): cross-platform command map — one task per row, the matching command on each platform, `null` where a platform documents none
- [Troubleshooting](/api/fehler.en.json): real error messages with cause, fix steps, prevention and sources
- [News feed](/api/feed.en.json): curated AI news, every item sourced
- [Comparison](/api/vergleiche.en.json): intelligence-per-dollar quadrant, price/performance ratio table, provider profiles
- [Prompts](/api/prompts.en.json): reusable prompt templates with placeholders
- [Add-ons](/api/addons.en.json): add-ons and MCP servers with setup steps from official docs
- [Benchmarks](/api/benchmarks.en.json): what each benchmark measures and how to read it
- [Timeline](/api/timeline.en.json): model releases and deprecations
- [Loop gallery](/api/loops.en.json): annotated good/bad agent loop examples

## Feeds

- [RSS (en)](/feed.en.xml): news feed as RSS 2.0 — also /feed.de.xml /feed.es.xml /feed.fr.xml /feed.zh.xml, and /feed.xml (German default)
- [Sitemap](/sitemap.xml): every page in every language

## Pages

- [Home (en)](/en/): start here; language variants at /de/ /es/ /fr/ /zh/
- [Start wizard](/en/start/): four questions, then a personal learning path plus a ready-to-copy project instruction file (CLAUDE.md, .cursor/rules, CONVENTIONS.md or AGENTS.md) and the first commands for your tool
- [Glossary](/en/lexikon/): all 111 chapters, each with a beginner and a deep version
- [Learning paths](/en/lernpfade/): four worlds as ordered chapter sequences
- [Command reference](/en/befehle/): 318 commands, grouped by platform
- [Command Rosetta](/en/rosetta/): the same task on all five platforms, side by side
- [Troubleshooting](/en/fehler/): searchable catalog of error messages and fixes
- [Comparison](/en/vergleiche/): which model is worth its price, dated and sourced
- [Timeline](/en/timeline/): model releases and deprecations
- [Feed](/en/feed/): current AI news, every claim sourced
- [Prompts](/en/prompts/): prompt templates with a fill-in sandbox
- [Add-ons](/en/addons/): MCP servers and extensions with setup
- [Benchmarks](/en/benchmarks/): how to read agent benchmarks
- [Daily challenge](/en/challenge/): five quiz questions per day, streak-based
- [Progress](/en/fortschritt/): personal XP, worlds and challenge record (stored in the browser only)
- [Loop gallery](/en/loops/): what a good agent loop looks like
- [Forum](/en/forum/): questions and discussion

## Notes

- Every entry cites its sources visibly; the platform never publishes unsourced claims. Sources are re-verified periodically — the last full link audit checked 410 URLs.
- Where a platform has no documented command for a task, the Rosetta map says so instead of guessing.
- Chapters come in two depth levels: `body` for beginners, `bodyDetail` for the mechanics.
- Report errors via the bug button on any page.
